EX 3 3 H – FSMA RF Picking for Active McLane sells “Single Sell , ” “Repack , ” and other smaller items which are slotted in WMOS Active Pick Locations. These items are picked into physical totes that act as an oLPN, typically containing many items in one tote. Picking in these areas occurs either in Dematic Pick to Light or by Pick Sheet . As part of FSMA compliance , GS1 barcodes and Lots are captured during picking. WMOS base lot tracking capabilities support tracking of lot through inbound processes, outbound processes, or both. McLane requires the ability to capture unique GS1 barcodes and lots during picking. T he display s of screens throughout the transaction are customized to meet McLane’s FSMA requests. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF N Reporting N Other N The custom Changes are made on top of EX33H FSMA RF Picking . EX33H enables attribute tracking during the custom EX 33 packing process. If an Item on the task detail is marked as a FSMA item (ITEM_WMS. BATCH_REQD = 1 ) then WMOS ensures a user scans a GS1 barcode , lot, and confirms quantity . The user will input the quantity for the GS1 barcode/lot. If there are more units to pick with a different GS1 barcode/lot, the user can repeat the process or a t this point, the user can short quantity from the Pick Location. WM OS captures each unique item barcode scanned. A custom error message is displayed if a user does not scan a valid GS1 barcode for a FSMA item. A custom control key is added to give the user the ability to scan the Location b arcode instead of the Item Barcode i f the GS1 barcode is unusable. If the user proceeds past the lot prompt and has not completely shorted the task detail , they are presented with the oLPN Confirmation scan(s). WMOS then proceeds to EX33 RF Picking for Active flow. Assumptions This extension modifies the Pack Pick Cart transaction only . Base configuration for shortage reason code is configured to default without prompting the user. Cigarette Picking does not occur through Pack Pick Cart . The custom changes described in this document works only in conjunction with extension EX33 – RF Picking for Active The scope of the change described in this document is only for RF Pack Pi ck Cart McLane Host/Hub utilizes the Item master (ITEM_WMS. BATCH_REQD) to identify items that require tracking. RF Pack Pick Cart program is configured to not prompt for item attributes including lot, product status and country of origin. GS1-128 scan compatible RF device with an ability to insert prefixes and separators within GS1-128 barcode are used by McLane. Single RF scan per unique GS1/Lot is assumed for the GS1-128 scan described in this document. The scanned GS1-128 barcode will not exceed 48 characters. This excludes the EAN prefixes and separators added by the RF device. All GS1-128 scan eligible items are configured with item GTIN as XREF barcode. All base supported Application Identifiers (AI) are configured in WMOS. Pack Pick Cart Transaction Parameter 4-4 is set =’ 4 ’ , “ Scan Item, Enter Quantity” New LPN Detail record created to hold each unique GS1 and Lot scan. Unique GS1 barcodes per oLPN will be captured if multiples are scanned. McLane owns the device modernization for full case picking. Dependencies Code Dependencies EX3 3 – RF Picking for Active EX35 – RF User Short Auto-A djust Inventory Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement ITEM_WMS ITEM_WMS à BATCH_REQD Sent to identify the item requires GS1 and lot tracking P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Process Flow Diagram FSMA Picking Process Steps User initiates work in the appropriate task group by requesting work from WMOS through CTRL -S in the RF main menu. If the packing task assigned is not an EX33 packing task, then base process continues, otherwise WMOS proceeds with EX33 custom processing. WMOS is customized to do a user level check for the total number of totes allowed to be worked with at a time. Base oLPN Size /Type screen is displayed, directing the user how many totes are needed for the task. Custom LPN.XREF_OLPN prompt displays to scan cross reference. This prompt repeats until all oLPNs on the task have a cross reference populated. Base oLPN Shipping Labels are printed in slot order ascending. WMOS directs the user to the Pick Location and prompts to confirm Item Barcode. If user takes exception control key for Location prompt, WMOS prompts the user for Location Barcode scan . Upon confirming Location , WMOS proceeds to Lot prompt. The user is able to completely short the task detail through base processing at this point through control key. If this process is taken, the user accepts the short message and be brought back to step 3 for a new task detail or the task ends. Otherwise, user scans Item Barcode to confirm and WMOS proceeds to the lot prompt. WMOS directs user to scan lot or confirm lot that was in the GS1 item barcode. Upon confirming/entering lot user proceeds to total quantity prompt. At the total quantity prompt, the user enters the amount of units for the GS1 barcode/lot. The user is able to enter a partial quantity here to partially short the task detail. If this process is taken, they are returned to the item prompt, now prompting for the remaining quantity, and execute short control key to short the remaining picks . If a partial quantity is packed, the user proceed s to step 6. If the user packs out the entire quantity, the user proceeds to step 9 and is prompted for oLPN confirmation. WMOS prompts for user for oLPN confirmation. If quantity picked from location is being packed into a single oLPN , WMOS is customized to not prompt for oLPN confirmation screen, and the user is sent back to step 3 for the next task detail or the task ends. If a quantity picked from the location is being packed into multiple oLPNs: The user is prompted to confirm the oLPN or GLP via pressing enter on the auto-populated qty tote number. The oLPN confirmation screen gives oLPN or LPN.XREF_OLPN information if it is populated and shows the qty needed in each tote prompt with the expected tote highlighted and already in the user input field. If oLPN/GLP is confirmed, the user returns to step 3 for a new task detail or the task ends. Else, total number of totes is more than 3, user hits enter to confirm first 3 puts into first 3 totes and is taken to the next screen for the next 3 totes to co nfirm puts. Upon all puts being confirmed WM then makes all expected updates. UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No changes to base behavior for PIX generation as part of this extension. RF RF Pack Pick Cart Program Platform Available Text Based (RF Gun) Y UI (RF UI) Y Mobile (DM) N Base RF Pack Pick Cart transaction is enhanced with display changes and pre-population of total quantity values. This transaction is also enhanced to sort task details of a new task to minimize physical movement. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing occur s . Second , the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX33H byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX33 H is turned off, and no custom EX33 H processing occurs. Lastly, the Custom RF transaction parameter must be enabled. If base transaction parameter for Active Verification Mode is set to ‘2’ – ‘Scan Item, Enter Quantity’ , custom transaction parameter 5 3 6 - 5 3 6 is set to “Y”, then the custom functionality within this section is enabled. If custom transaction parameter 5 3 6 - 5 3 6 is set to “N”, or not set up at all, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with EX33 processing . . Validations WMOS first performs custom validations to identify if the scanned barcode is populated with EAN prefix configured for the warehouse. If not WMOS displays custom error message CUST/ 3330 “Invalid GS1-128 barcode”. Otherwise, WMOS proceeds with GS1-128 barcode parsing and validations per base and captures following AI values in session. The AI capture includes some fields not supported in base RF Pack Pick Cart . Application Identifier Format Description 00 18 byte – Fixed Serial Shipping Container Code (SSCC). This is LPN header information but customized to capture for LPN detail. 01 14 byte – Fixed Item GTIN (Global Trade Item Number). This field is used for validation since it’s already configured as WMOS item master XREF barcode. 02 14 byte – Fixed Item GTIN (Global Trade Item Number). This field is used for validation since it’s already configured as WMOS item master XREF barcode. 10 20 byte – Variable Lot/batch number. 11 6 byte – Fixed ( YYMMDD) Manufacturing date. This is LPN header information but customized to capture for LPN detail. 13 6 byte – Fixed ( YYMMDD) Packaging Date. 15 6 byte – Fixed ( YYMMDD) Sell by /Ship by date. This is LPN header information but customized to capture for LPN detail. 17 6 byte – Fixed ( YYMMDD) Expiry date. This is LPN header information but customized to capture for LPN detail. 310 6 byte – Variable Catch Weight (kg) 320 6 byte – Variable Catch Weight ( lbs ) 422 3 bytes – Fixed Country of Origin. Then WMOS performs the base validations for Item confirmation. If any of these validations fail, the base error displays and no custom logic is required. WMOS validates that the GS1 barcode scanned does not already exist on the oLPN. If full short/partial short functionality is invoked and If there are open replenishments in released/in progress/locked/disabled for the item attempting to short, then EX33 base shorting logic is invoked if EX35 Replenishment Functionality (transaction parameter 505-505) for RF Pack Pick Cart transaction is turned off, then EX33 base shorting logic is invoked. else if EX35 Replenishment Functionality (transaction parameter 505-505) is turned on for RF Pack Pick Cart transaction and does not have any replenishments in released/in progress/locked/disabled, then EX35 custom replenishment logic (EX35 mod spec section 7.1) is invoked. The existing oLPN detail is updated if the GS1 Barcode scanned already exists. If the GS1 barcode scanned is unique a new LPN detail is added. *Technical Note: Base Alternate /skip logic is utilized to create new task detail and oLPN detai l . * Navigation Upon successfully capturing the item, after packing completion WMOS proceeds to the Lot prompt. If the user presses the CTRL-A Toggle key, WMOS navigates to the base Item Components prompt . If the user presses CTRL-A again, WMOS navigates to the base Item Name prompt. Upon successfully capturing the Item Name, after packing co mpletion WMOS proceeds to the Lot prompt. If the user presses CTRL -D to short, after completing shortage updates, WMOS continues to the next detail. If the user presses CTRL -L for Location Prompt, WMOS navigates to screen 2 – Location Prompt. If the user presses CTRL -X to exit on this screen, WMOS returns the operator to the RF main menu. If the user presses CTRL -K to skip, WMOS proceeds to the next task detail. If the user presses CTRL -W to move to previous screen, WMOS moves back to the LPN Size/Type screen. If the user keys CTRL -E to end oLPN, WMOS follows custom flow below (For further information refer to sec. 6.1.12). THEN; User is unable to pick any units into original oLPN for current item, WMOS bypasses oLPN confirmation tote screen unless items already picked into oLPN . AND; WMOS creates new oLPN via base, user is directed back to screen 1 looping through the custom EX33 screen flow (i.e. user scans new cross ref erence oLPN, item prompt and then q uantity prompt). *Technical Note: EX33 is customized to invoke CTRL -e on aggregate q uanti ty and multi-item flow* Outputs Activity Tracking No changes to the base activity tracking as part of this extension. If the user keys CTRL-N to generate a lot, WMOS will capture the LPN_DETAIL.BATCH_NUMBER as the Last lot associated to the last iLPN putaway to the current pick location as the Lot(Select BATCH_NBR from PROD_TRKG_TRAN PTT Inner Join LPN L On PTT.CNTR_NBR = L.TC_LPN_ID Inner join LPN_DETAIL LD on L.LPN_ID = LD.LPN_ID where TO_LOCN = 'Current Pick location’ and MODULE_NAME = 'Replenish' order by MOD_DATE_TIME desc; ), and proceeds to the Total Quantity Prompt screen . Note: In the even t that the last iLPN putaway to the active location, does not have a lot, WMOS continues to search the next iLPNs putaway until it finds the most recent iLPN with a lot for that pick location. If the query above does not return a valid Lot number, then the oLPN number (LPN.TC_LPN_ID) should be used to populate the Lot prompt and serve as the oLPN’s batch number. EX 3 3 H – FSMA RF Picking for Active McLane sells “Single Sell , ” “Repack , ” and other smaller items which are slotted in WMOS Active Pick Locations. These items are picked into physical totes that act as an oLPN, typically containing many items in one tote. Picking in these areas occurs either in Dematic Pick to Light or by Pick Sheet . As part of FSMA compliance , GS1 barcodes and Lots are captured during picking. WMOS base lot tracking capabilities support tracking of lot through inbound processes, outbound processes, or both. McLane requires the ability to capture unique GS1 barcodes and lots during picking.

Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF N Reporting N Other N The custom Changes are made on top of EX33H FSMA RF Picking . EX33H enables attribute tracking during the custom EX 33 packing process. If an Item on the task detail is marked as a FSMA item (ITEM_WMS. BATCH_REQD = 1 ) then WMOS ensures a user scans a GS1 barcode , lot, and confirms quantity . The user will input the quantity for the GS1 barcode/lot. If there are more units to pick with a different GS1 barcode/lot, the user can repeat the process or a t this point, the user can short quantity from the Pick Location. WM OS captures each unique item barcode scanned. A custom error message is displayed if a user does not scan a valid GS1 barcode for a FSMA item. A custom control key is added to give the user the ability to scan the Location b arcode instead of the Item Barcode i f the GS1 barcode is unusable. If the user proceeds past the lot prompt and has not completely shorted the task detail , they are presented with the oLPN Confirmation scan(s). WMOS then proceeds to EX33 RF Picking for Active flow. Assumptions This extension modifies the Pack Pick Cart transaction only . Base configuration for shortage reason code is configured to default without prompting the user. Cigarette Picking does not occur through Pack Pick Cart . The custom changes described in this document works only in conjunction with extension EX33 – RF Picking for Active The scope of the change described in this document is only for RF Pack Pi ck Cart McLane Host/Hub utilizes the Item master (ITEM_WMS. BATCH_REQD) to identify items that require tracking. RF Pack Pick Cart program is configured to not prompt for item attributes including lot, product status and country of origin. GS1-128 scan compatible RF device with an ability to insert prefixes and separators within GS1-128 barcode are used by McLane. Single RF scan per unique GS1/Lot is assumed for the GS1-128 scan described in this document. The scanned GS1-128 barcode will not exceed 48 characters. This excludes the EAN prefixes and separators added by the RF device. All GS1-128 scan eligible items are configured with item GTIN as XREF barcode. All base supported Application Identifiers (AI) are configured in WMOS.

If the query above does not return a valid Lot number, then the oLPN number (LPN.TC_LPN_ID) should be used to populate the Lot prompt and serve as the oLPN’s batch number. icks from a Pick Location into one Total Quantity Prompt if there are multiple details being packed into multiple oLPNs. If the Total Quantity Prompt is being pulled from a single task detail, instead of aggregating multiple task details, McLane preference is to avoid the oLPN confirmation screen.

Base typically can handle this, however, when enabling oLPN Cross Reference scanning support, our configuration forces the oLPN scan in this situation. Therefore, WMOS is enhanced to avoid this oLPN confirmation screen in a single oLPN scenario (For further information refer to EX33 RF Picking for Active sec. 6.1. 8 ) IF transaction parameter 517-517 byte 1-1 =’N’ And the total quantity from the pick location is being packed into one oLPN Then skip the oLPN confirmation screen and perform packing updates, proceed to next task detail If transaction parameter 517-517 byte 1-1 =’Y’ And the total quantity from the pick location is being packed into one oLPN And the LPN.XREF_OLPN is null Then proceed with base oLPN confirmation screen Else, skip oLPN confirmation screen and perform packing updates, proceed to next task detail Navigation If WMOS is aggregating multiple task details into one Total Quantity Prompt and the user enters the expected total quantity for tha t GS1 barcode/lot , WMOS proceeds to the oLPN confirmation screen. If user scans quantity less than aggregated quantity, then WMOS proceeds to the Item Prompt. If WMOS is displaying a single task detail in the Total Quantity Prompt, WMOS skips the oLPN confirmation and proceeds to the next task detail. If base WMOS concludes that the oLPN being packed out has been packed complete and circumstances have skipped the oLPN Confirmation screen, logic in 6.1.9.3 determines whether warning message CUST/3301 End of oLPN or CUST/3302 is shown. If user enters partial quantity, WMOS returns to screen 1 Item Prompt with the packed quantity decremented . If the user keys CTRL-D to short, after completing shortage updates, WMOS proceeds to the next task detail. If the user keys CTRL-K to skip, the user skips the current task detail. If the user keys CTRL-W for previous, the user moves to the previous screen (Screen 3 – Lot Prompt). If the user keys CTRL-X to exit, WMOS returns the user to the RF Main Menu If the user keys CTRL-E to end oLPN, WMOS follows custom flow described in EX33 (For further information refer to EX33 RF Picking for Active sec. 6.1.12). THEN; IF; User picks partial quantity WMOS moves into EX33 and displays custom oLPN confirmation prompt ( For further information refer to EX33 RF Picking for Active sec. 6.1. 9 ) with partial quantity picked into original oLPN.
